sound_type: body-slap breach -- 40-ton animal launching out of the water and crashing back. ~10,000 watts acoustic energy on impact. Broadband percussive. E=7
ecosystem: Near shore, continental shelf, migration corridor. Washington coast. One of the most visible whale migration routes.
season: Spring/fall migration. Some year-round residents in Washington waters.

coordinates.description: Gray Whale along Washington outer coast migration corridor. Near-shore breaching.

taxonomy_note.description: Second Gray Whale entry. First at 192 (blow, E=4). This entry covers the breach (E=7). HIGHEST SPS ENERGY. 14 consecutive marine mammals (180-193). 7 families.
taxonomy_note.family_count_in_engine: Eschrichtiidae: Gray Whale Blow (192, E=4), Gray Whale Breach (193, E=7).
taxonomy_note.name_etymology: Eschrichtius from Daniel Eschricht (Danish zoologist). robustus from Latin 'strong/robust'. 'Gray Whale' for its mottled gray coloring.
taxonomy_note.genus_note: Eschrichtius -- monotypic genus. Only one living species.
taxonomy_note.order_note: Cetacea. Fourth cetacean family in SPS.
taxonomy_note.family_position: Seventh marine mammal family: Phocidae, Otariidae, Mustelidae, Phocoenidae, Delphinidae, Balaenopteridae, Eschrichtiidae.
taxonomy_note.class_note: Class Mammalia. Marine mammal era: 14 consecutive (180-193). LONGEST STREAK IN ENGINE HISTORY.

song_metrics.fundamental_frequency_range_hz: 20-5,000 Hz. Broadband percussive. Peak energy 20-200 Hz.
song_metrics.song_frequency_hz: Broadband: 20-5,000 Hz. Not a vocalization -- physical impact sound.
song_metrics.song_duration_s: Impact: 0.5-2 sec. Reverberant decay: 5-10 sec underwater. Splash: 3-5 sec above water.
song_metrics.note_structure: Single massive broadband percussive impulse per breach. May repeat 20-40 times in series.
song_metrics.phrase_structure: Breach series: 20-40 breaches over 30+ minutes. Each breach a single percussive event.
song_metrics.timbre: Massive percussive slap. Broadband. Low-frequency dominant. Like a building hitting water.
song_metrics.amplitude: E=7. ~10,000 watts acoustic energy per impact. HIGHEST SPS ENERGY. Loudest single event in engine.
song_metrics.tempo: Breach series: 1-3 minutes between breaches. Variable.
song_metrics.context: Near shore, migration corridor, open water. Communication, parasite removal, play, social display.
song_metrics.regional_variation: Breach frequency varies by population and season. Resident whales may breach less than migrating.
song_metrics.key_property: BODY-SLAP BREACH. 40 TONS AIRBORNE. ~10,000 WATTS. E=7 HIGHEST SPS. Second Gray Whale (192 blow). 14 consecutive marine mammals. v1.49.334.

pelage.overall: Mottled dark and light gray. Heavily encrusted with barnacles and whale lice (cyamids). No dorsal fin -- low dorsal hump followed by 6-12 knuckles along dorsal ridge.
pelage.diagnostic_mark: Mottled gray. Heavy barnacle/cyamid encrustation. No dorsal fin. Heart-shaped blow (double blowhole). Knuckled dorsal ridge.
pelage.seasonal_variation: Cyamid load varies. Barnacle encrustation accumulates over time. Calves smoother than adults.
pelage.swimming_pattern: Slow cruiser (3-5 mph migration). Breach: explosive vertical launch, 40 tons airborne, massive splash on re-entry.

physical_description.overall: Length: 13-15 m (43-49 ft). Weight: 30-40 tons. Robust body. Mottled gray. No dorsal fin. Baleen plates for filter-feeding on benthic amphipods.
physical_description.diagnostic_mark: Mottled gray with barnacles and cyamids. No dorsal fin. Heart-shaped blow. Knuckled dorsal ridge. Breach visible from miles.
physical_description.field_id: Large mottled gray whale, no dorsal fin, heavy barnacle encrustation. Near shore. Breach: entire body airborne, massive splash. Heart-shaped blow from double blowhole.

nodes.title: E=7 -- Highest SPS Energy
nodes.description: New SPS energy record. ~10,000 watts on impact. Surpasses Steller Sea Lion (E=6). The breach is the loudest single-event sound in the engine.

nodes.title: Breach Mechanics -- 40-Ton Launch
nodes.description: 5-8 m/s exit velocity. 500K-1M newtons impact. 0.5-1% daily energy per breach. Repeated 20-40 times. The physics of a whale going airborne.

nodes.title: Blow to Breach -- E=4 to E=7
nodes.description: Same species across 192-193. Blow (exhalation, E=4) to breach (body-slap, E=7). +3 energy. Two acoustic faces of the same whale.

nodes.title: 14 Consecutive Marine Mammals
nodes.description: Degrees 180-193. 14 entries. 7 families. E=1 to E=7. The longest streak in engine history.

cross_references.relationship: SECOND GRAY WHALE! Blow (E=4) to breach (E=7). Same species, +3 energy. Two acoustic faces.
WHALE PROGRESSION! Song (E=6) to breach (E=7). Vocalization to physical percussion.
PREVIOUS RECORD! Sea lion colony roar E=6. Breach surpasses it at E=7.
FOUNDATION AND SPECTACLE! Fastbacks (since 1979, foundation) + breach (most spectacular event). Total E=12.
